item.ptxt: Ignition timing is not adjustable. If ignition timing is not as specified, check components related to ignition system.
For identification of each cylinder, refer to Statement on Cleanliness and Care .
Before starting engine, place gear shift lever in “Neutral” (M/T model) or select “P” range (A/T model), and set parking brake.

s1.ptxt: Connect SUZUKI scan tool to DLC (1) with ignition “OFF”.
Start engine and warm it up to normal operating temperature.
Make sure that all electrical loads except ignition system are switched off.
Check that idle speed is within specification referring to Idle Speed and IAC Throttle Valve Opening Inspection .
Fix ignition timing by using “Fixed Spark” under “Active Test” mode on SUZUKI scan tool.
Set timing light (1) to ignition coil harness for No.1 cylinder and check that ignition timing is within specification.
If ignition timing is out of specification, check the following points.

item.ptxt: CMP sensor rotor tooth of camshaft
Vehicle speed signal from ABS / ESP® hydraulic unit / control module

s1.ptxt: After checking initial ignition timing, release ignition timing fixation by using SUZUKI scan tool.

With engine idling, check that ignition timing is about 10 ± 5° BTDC (Constant variation within a few degrees from 10 ± 5° BTDC indicates no abnormality but proves operation of electronic timing control system.) Also, check that increasing engine speed advances ignition timing.
If the check results are not satisfactory, check CKP sensor and ECM.
